programmes.show_description: All Day Dining. Guy visits a gastropub marrying pork belly with octopus. And in Wyoming, he visits a log cabin-turned restaurant cooking up a banana bread French toast mash-up. S20, Ep5
All Things New York. Guy Fieri gets a taste of NYC. In Eureka, CA, a Bronx-bred pizza maker brings Neapolitan-style pies. In Queens, a filmmaker-turned-chef serves atomic fireballs. S19, Ep2
Big Time Bites. In New York's East Village, Guy Fieri visits the neighbourhood spot plating up a standout burger. In Eureka, CA a popular hangout serves roasted oysters. S19, Ep3
Keepin' It Real. In Miami, Guy visits the Asian fusion spot giving Brussels sprouts a makeover. In Eureka, CA, a soul food spot serves up favourites like jerk fried chicken. S19, Ep4
Cincinnati. Adam head to Cincinnati, Ohio where he devours some grilled cheese sandwiches. Later, he guides namesake Adam through Reuben Challenge at Izzy's, but will he succeed? S1, Ep20
Nascar. Adam heads to Rochester, New York via a NASCAR race. Later, he guides driver Joey Logano through the Atomic Bomb Challenge, involving a three-pound cheeseburger. S1, Ep21
Secret Proposal, Patients And Privacy Please. Buddy gets a visit from Leah, a seven-year-old baker he met in Malaysia. Mauro and Madeline help a young man propose to his sweetheart. S9, Ep15
Enduring Love, An Ending And Edited Out. Buddy recreates a 6-tier wedding cake for Lisa's grandparents' anniversary. Plus, Grace ruffles some feathers with a new training video. S9, Ep16
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Rick visits a free-range goose farm in Leicester, inspiring him to tackle a roast goose dish. Plus, he visits the oldest eel and pie shop in London. S1, Ep3
Rick experiences the Lake District's quality produce. He cooks a Chinese-inspired wild boar stew. Plus, he meets a farmer who keeps Herdwick sheep. S1, Ep4
Cumbria. The Bikers tour the Lake District, starting in Dave's hometown Barrow-in-Furness for a trip down memory lane before visiting a bakery loved by the local community. S1, Ep3
Sweet Talk. Ree Drummond celebrates the opening of her new sweet shop. She makes an incredible jewelled divinity and two over-the-top ice cream sundaes. S23, Ep9
Moving House. Ree Drummond makes a dinner to-go for a new cowboy on the ranch. The main course is a chicken jalapeno popper casserole followed by pretzel rolls and watermelon. S23, Ep10
Scallops, Steak, Flat Breads. Chef Jamie Oliver shows off his speedy cooking by preparing seared scallops. Plus, he serves up delicious rib-eye steak and almond puff. S1, Ep1
Tuna, Meatballs, Cookies. Jamie demonstrates fast and tasty cooking by searing a mouth-watering sesame tuna steak. Then, he makes messy meatball buns and chocolate rye cookies. S1, Ep2
Eat Your Greens. Ina shares vegetable ideas for every day of the week. She prepares dishes including spring green risotto with and warm mushroom salad. S7, Ep5
The Adventure Begins. Ina and Jeffrey take a trip to California. For the first meal she cooks chicken with wild mushrooms. Later, she makes baked goat cheese and grilled pork chops. S8, Ep1
A new set of bakers enter the kitchen and their first Signature Challenge is to make a Swiss roll, and then they are faced with the technical challenge of baking a classic cherry cake. S5, Ep1
The remaining 11 bakers must push themselves on flavour to create biscuits that go well with a cheese course. And later, they are asked to make Florentines. S5, Ep2
James's adventure takes him to the Camargue. He learns of the fleur de sel, a delicate salty crust which is harvested in the waters of Aigues Mortes. S1, Ep13
James journeys to Arles, famous for its Roman architecture and home to Vincent Van Gogh. He cooks a delicious dish using food from the local market stalls. S1, Ep14

With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1, Ep2
Jamie cooks up his version of steak and onion sandwich and shows his way to cook osso buco. Plus, he makes a sticky toffee pudding with his mum. S1, Ep3
Jamie serves up three comfort food classics: chicken kiev, shepherd's pie and spaghetti alle vongole with a non-traditional pink twist. S1, Ep4
Dynamite Duos. Guy Fieri checks out spots keeping it in the family. In New York, a sibling spot pickles, smokes, cures and ferments everything from goat necks to pig ears. S19, Ep5
Kickin' It In Key West. Guy Fieri visits eateries in Key West, FLA, from a funky little shack serving up New England seafood to a tiny food cart cranking out Asian-Caribbean dishes. S19, Ep6
From Meatballs To Lollipops. Guy Fieri gets into a festival of flavours. In Salt Lake City, a New Jersey native serves up Italian family recipes like the huge ten-ounce meatball! S19, Ep7
Cross Country Comfort Food. Guy Fieri seeks comfort food from the slopes to the sea. In Utah, a local boy serves a gourmet chivito club and a ski lodge dishes out crispy pork belly. S19, Ep8
Omaha. Adam heads to Omaha, Nebraska where he samples the famous Godfather sandwich at Amato's Café. Later he guides Matt through the Pig Wing Challenge, but will he succeed? S1, Ep22
Oahu. Adam heads to Oahu, where he tries a Chicago style frank with a Hawaiian twist. Later, he guides Lauren through The Moose Omelette Challenge, but will she succeed? S1, Ep24
Holly Jolly Hoboken and Ugly Sweaters. It's the holiday season, and team makes a cake for the New York Botanical Garden train show. Plus, the team creates an 'ugly sweater' cake. S9, Ep17
Piñata Party And Gender Reveal. Buddy is challenged to create a piñata cake by two 11-year-old DJs. Plus, two expectant mothers order gender-reveal cakes - will it be pink or blue? S9, Ep18

With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1, Ep2
North Yorkshire. Si and Dave ride through the North York Moors National Park. They meet the UK's youngest Michelin-star winning chef, an award-winning beekeeper and a local forager. S1, Ep4
Meg's Getting Married. Ree Drummond recreates a Tex-Mex dish from a friend's engagement party. She makes crispy cerveza battered fish and prickly pear margaritas. S23, Ep11
Thanksgiving Potluck. Ree Drummond showcases her Thanksgiving favourites in fantastic fashion. For the main dish she cooks an amazing glazed ham and for dessert a pumpkin rum cake. S23, Ep12
Fishcakes, Linguine, Lamb Chops. Jamie Oliver cooks up quick Asian fishcakes with a sweet and spicy glaze. Then, he serves up lemon and courgette linguine and sticky lamb chops. S1, Ep3
Lamb, Spaghetti, Fish, Cookies. Jamie cooks tender lamb shoulder with an easy cheat for maximum flavour. Plus, he prepares his speedy veggie spaghetti and apple crumble cookies. S1, Ep4
Off Duty. Ina sees what professional cooks make at home when they're off duty. Recipes include banana raspberry smoothies and kimchi fried rice with fried egg. S7, Ep7
Restaurant Rules. Ina shares restaurant-inspired dishes and secrets from America's best kitchens. On the menu are peas and prosciutto, lemon chicken and skillet brownies. S7, Ep8
In week three, the remaining ten bakers get ready to brave bread. The boys are confident that this is their week, but the girls have other ideas. S5, Ep3
Having seen the bakers make cake, biscuits and bread, Mary and Paul up the ante - for the first time, we see how the bakers cope with multitasking across several baking skills at once. S5, Ep4
James heads to the riverside town of Isle Sur La Sorge, where his food hero, Keith Floyd, once owned a restaurant. He cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. S1, Ep15
James travels to Normandy, home to his all-time favourite ingredient, butter! He cooks up artichokes with hollandaise whilst visiting a Calvados orchard. S1, Ep16

Best Of City Bakes - Amazing Bakers. Paul Hollywood showcases some of the most inspirational characters that opened his eyes to the depth of baking heritage. S1, Ep12
Best Of City Bakes - Incredible Bakeries. Paul looks back over his journey around the globe and showcases some of his favourite moments spent with the bakers themselves. S1, Ep13
